promise_under_test: DC2 — an out-of-authority effect is REJECTED before it touches the WAL, not merely logged after the fact. Differentiator vs Python v2 (which claimed append-only but derived state from hand-edited files).

scenario.setup: a target file .forge-method/artifacts/target.txt exists with content 'v1'; an empty WAL ledger.ndjson is initialized
scenario.attack: an effect attempts a Write over the target, asserting a stale/foreign expected_hash (content the caller never observed). This is exactly an out-of-authority write: the caller has no fresh authority over the current on-disk state.
scenario.expected: the freshness check fails in prepare_file_writes (pre-flight), the transaction is Blocked BEFORE any WAL append, and state is unchanged.

2_typed_reason_no_panic.assertion: result.reasons contains EffectApplicationReason::ExpectedHashMismatch (typed enum reason_code, never a panic)

3a_target_unchanged.assertion: target file content still equals 'v1' (hash matches pre-attempt)

3b_wal_unchanged.assertion: WAL sha256 + line count byte-for-byte identical before vs after the rejected attempt (the core promise)

mechanism_chain_verified: append_effect_wal_record is NEVER reached (the WAL append sits AFTER the pre-flight block)

acceptance_criteria.out-of-authority effect rejected, state: PASS — target + WAL both byte-identical after attempt

exit_signal: S0.2 green + S0.3 audit done + S0.4 demo green == SLICE 0 COMPLETE. Spine proven healthy (Option A: reuse not rewrite confirmed).
next_workflow: plan slice 1 in detail, then build S1.1
